title: Lively Argentinian Beef Stew
description: A fragrant, low-carb stew infused with ghee and a classic mirepoix base.
instructions: Heat the ghee in a large pot over medium-high heat until shimmering.
Add the beef cubes and sear each side until browned, about 3 minutes per side.
Remove the beef and set aside, then lower the heat to medium to reduce grease if necessary.
Add the diced onion, carrot, and celery to the pot and cook for 5 minutes until softened.
Stir in the minced garlic, oregano, paprika, cumin, and bay leaf, cooking for 1 minute until fragrant.
Pour in the red wine to deglaze, scraping up brown bits, and let it reduce by half, about 3 minutes.
Return the beef to the pot along with tomato paste, beef broth, salt, and pepper.
Bring the mixture to a gentle simmer, cover, and cook on low heat for 65 minutes until the beef is tender.
Uncover, stir, and continue to simmer for another 10 minutes to thicken the sauce.
Garnish with chopped parsley before serving hot.
